steps.title: Read the support relationship
steps.content: The first sentence says Chaucer pioneered the literary use of English at a time when English was rarely used in literature. The next sentence strengthens that claim by pointing to more than 2,000 first documented uses of English words in his manuscripts.

steps.title: Choose emphasis, not contrast
steps.content: "In fact" introduces evidence that supports and intensifies the previous statement, which fits the example of Chaucer's documented word uses. "However" and "On the contrary" would signal disagreement with the first sentence, but the second sentence supports it. "Besides" would add a separate point without the same emphatic support. The answer is C.
